title: Suspicious sign-in investigation
prompt: A synthetic alert shows repeated failed sign-ins followed by a successful login from an unfamiliar location and a mailbox rule change.

artifacts.name: Synthetic Entra sign-in events
artifacts.content:
2026-06-05T09:12:04Z user=alex.chen@example.com ip=203.0.113.42 result=Failure reason=InvalidPassword
2026-06-05T09:18:44Z user=alex.chen@example.com ip=203.0.113.42 result=Failure reason=InvalidPassword
2026-06-05T09:26:11Z user=alex.chen@example.com ip=198.51.100.17 result=Success location=Unfamiliar

artifacts.name: Synthetic endpoint note
artifacts.content: Host=WKSTN-014 severity=Medium observation=No malware detected; browser session token reuse suspected.

questions.prompt: What is the most likely incident type?

options.label: Benign travel
options.text: Treat as normal travel and close the alert.

options.label: Endpoint malware
options.text: Treat as endpoint malware without checking identity artifacts.

questions.explanation: The failed sign-ins, unfamiliar successful login, and forwarding rule point to likely account compromise.

citations.title: Synthetic Suspicious Sign-in Lab
citations.excerpt: The lab uses documentation-reserved IP addresses and fictional identities to simulate suspicious sign-in triage.

questions.prompt: Which containment actions should happen first?

options.label: Revoke sessions
options.text: Revoke active sessions for the affected synthetic user.

options.label: Reset password
options.text: Require a password reset for the affected synthetic user.

questions.explanation: Containment should revoke sessions, reset credentials, and remove the suspicious rule without destructive overreach.

citations.title: Synthetic Incident Triage Playbook
citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.

questions.prompt: Write the evidence preservation note for the incident handoff.
questions.explanation: The handoff should preserve identity, mailbox, and endpoint context before drawing broad conclusions.

citations.title: Synthetic Incident Triage Playbook
citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.

learner_task: Identify the likely incident type, choose first containment actions, and write the evidence that should be preserved.
expected_investigation_path: Treat the pattern as suspected account compromise.
Preserve sign-in logs, mailbox audit events, and endpoint context.
Contain by requiring password reset, revoking active sessions, and disabling the suspicious forwarding rule.
Escalate with a concise incident handoff and note that all artifacts are synthetic.

rubric.expected_signal: Names suspected account compromise or identity-based intrusion.
Prioritizes session revocation, password reset, and mailbox rule removal.
Preserves sign-in, mailbox, and endpoint evidence before broad conclusions.

safety_note: Defensive synthetic lab only. Do not use these artifacts to infer real users, tenants, or systems.

citations.title: Synthetic Suspicious Sign-in Lab
citations.excerpt: The lab uses documentation-reserved IP addresses and fictional identities to simulate suspicious sign-in triage.

citations.title: Synthetic Incident Triage Playbook
citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.
